description_long: This object is a large commercial jet airliner with a streamlined fuselage and a pair of swept-back wings mounted low on the body. The aircraft features four turbofan engines, two mounted on each side of the rear fuselage, and a distinctive T-tail configuration with a horizontal stabilizer mounted atop the vertical fin. The fuselage is cylindrical with a row of passenger windows along each side, and the cockpit is positioned at the very front with multiple windows for visibility. The landing gear is of the tricycle type, with a single nose wheel and two main gear units under the wings. The exterior surface appears to be primarily metallic, finished in a light color with some darker accents around the engines and tail. The overall design suggests a focus on passenger transport, with ample space for boarding and seating inside.
description: A large, swept-wing commercial jet airliner with four rear-mounted turbofan engines, a T-tail, and a streamlined fuselage lined with passenger windows.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the four turbofan engines clustered at the back and the prominent T-tail configuration.
description_view_1: Left side profile highlighting the full length of the fuselage, the row of passenger windows, and the low-mounted swept wings.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the cockpit windows, nose landing gear, and the symmetrical sweep of the wings.
description_view_3: Right side profile similar to the left, showing the passenger windows, wing, and tail structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with swept-back wings and slight dihedral angle; no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Four rear-mounted turbofan engines, two on each side of the fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer mounted at the top.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with one nose gear and two main gear units.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows at the front of the fuselage.
